questions.question: Format of an Informal / Friendly Letter
questions.answer:
Sender's Address,
(House No., Street,
Area/Locality,
City,
Pin Code.)

Date (e.g., 23rd March, 2026)

My dear ______,

I am writing this letter to...

[Body - Para 1: Enquiry about the person's well-being]

[Body - Para 2: Friendly expression of feelings and thoughts]

[Conclusion - Take words from the question]

Your loving ______,
[First Name only]

---

IMPORTANT RULES:
1. Use 'My dear' (NOT just 'Dear') for family members
2. Address: Sender's address only (NO receiver's address)
3. Closing: 'Your loving son/daughter/friend' OR 'Yours affectionately' (for family), 'Yours lovingly' (for friends)
4. Sign with FIRST NAME only
5. NO subject line needed
6. Body should have 2-3 paragraphs with personal touch

questions.question: Format of a Formal Letter
questions.answer:
Sender's Address,
(House No., Street,
Area/Locality,
City - Pin Code.)

Date (e.g., 24th March, 2026)

Receiver's Address:
(Designation/Name,
Organisation/School,
Area,
City - Pin Code.)

Dear Madam/Sir,

Subject: [State the purpose clearly]

I am writing this letter to...

[Body - Para 1: Direct approach to purpose, 3-4 points with details]

[Body - Para 2: Additional information/supporting details]

[Conclusion - Awaiting a positive reply / Looking forward to your response]

Thanking you,
Yours truly,
[Full Name]

---

IMPORTANT RULES:
1. Use 'Dear Madam/Sir' (formal greeting)
2. Include BOTH sender's and receiver's addresses
3. Subject line is REQUIRED
4. Body: 3-4 points with specific details
5. Closing: 'Yours truly' or 'Yours sincerely' or 'Yours faithfully'
6. Sign with FULL NAME
7. Language must be polite and formal
8. Keep it to the point

questions.question: Format of a Formal Email
questions.answer:
From: [your email id]
To: [receiver's email id]
Subject: [subject of the email]

Salutation: Dear Ma'am/Sir,

[Body of the email]
Para 1: Direct approach to purpose
Para 2: Positively summing up with suggestions, action sought, or a commitment

Closing note:
Looking forward to your reply, / Looking forward to hearing from you.

Signature:
Yours sincerely,
[Full Name]
[Designation, if any]

---

STARTER PHRASES:
- I hope you are doing well.
- I hope this email finds you well.
- Allow me to introduce myself briefly.
- I am writing to...

CLOSING PHRASES:
- Looking forward to your reply.
- Looking forward to hearing from you.
- Please let me know if you have any questions.

questions.question: Key Differences: Informal vs Formal Letter
questions.answer:
INFORMAL LETTER:
- Written to family, friends, relatives
- Personal/casual tone with emotions
- Greeting: 'My dear ___'
- Only sender's address needed
- No subject line
- Closing: 'Your loving ___' / 'Yours affectionately'
- Sign with first name

FORMAL LETTER:
- Written to officials, institutions, organisations
- Polite and formal tone, to the point
- Greeting: 'Dear Madam/Sir'
- Both sender's and receiver's addresses needed
- Subject line is required
- Closing: 'Yours truly' / 'Yours sincerely' / 'Yours faithfully'
- Sign with full name

FORMAL EMAIL:
- Same as formal letter but in email format
- Has From, To, Subject fields
- No postal addresses needed
- Can include attachments
- Closing: 'Yours sincerely' + name + designation
